title: T-079 — Migration/API contract: factory_specs Technical-owned version approval
prompt:
# T-079 — Migration/API contract: factory_specs Technical-owned version approval

PRD: docs/prd/03-TECHNICAL-PRD.md §0, §5.1A, §7.4
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Implement/document Migration/API contract: factory_specs Technical-owned version approval for 03-TECHNICAL final-decision workflow.

This task establishes factory_spec as Technical-owned SSOT for production instructions. NPD Builder is allowed to call create draft with source npd_builder, but it cannot approve for factory use. Product-facing aliases may say internal_product_spec; table/API naming is factory_specs unless existing code conventions demand a thin alias. Approval must write audit_log entries. D365 imports may create draft or review records only. No application UI in this task.

## Implementation contract
See `## Goal` + scope_files + acceptance_criteria below; preserve prototype parity, server-action semantics, RLS/org-scoping, and module red-lines listed in `## Risk red lines`.

## Files
- db/migrations/0xxx_factory_specs.sql [create]
- db/schema/factory-specs.ts [create]
- apps/web/actions/technical/factory-specs/*.ts [create]
- tests/db/factory-specs.migration.test.ts [create]
- tests/actions/technical/factory-specs.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given an approved_for_factory factory_spec exists, when update-in-place is attempted, then the operation is rejected and audit_log records the rejection.
2. Given an approved_for_factory version v2 exists, when editReleasedFactorySpec is called, then version v3 is created with status draft and v2 remains unchanged.
3. Given NPD Builder creates an initial spec, when source=npd_builder, then status is draft and approval fields are empty.
4. Given a factory use check runs, when no approved_for_factory spec exists for FG/intermediate, then factory use is blocked with a typed reason.

## Test strategy
- Add RED tests for immutable approved rows and clone-on-edit before migration/service code.
- Run: pnpm vitest run tests/db/factory-specs.migration.test.ts tests/actions/technical/factory-specs.test.ts

## Risk red lines
- Do not merge factory_specs into items.ext_jsonb.
- Do not allow NPD roles to approve factory use.
- Do not make D365 authoritative for approved specs.
- FG is canonical; FA is legacy alias only — do not introduce FA-* identifiers in new schemas, code, labels, tests.
- D365 is optional integration; never use D365 IDs as hard FKs — d365_item_id is TEXT soft reference only.
- Released BOM/factory_spec edits clone-on-write a new version; never mutate an approved/released row in place.
- Shared BOM SSOT is canonical — Technical, NPD, Planning, Production, Warehouse and Finance read the same bom_headers/lines/co_products tables.
